title: stephanomeria occultata
intro: Stephanomeria occultata, also known as the secret ceanothus or secret Mormon tea, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Stephanomeria occultata include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Stephanomeria occultata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support respiratory health.
